meta_title: For Founders: portfolio building to Solve no project ideas (Advanced Idea List)
meta_description: Learn how to portfolio building and overcome no project ideas. A Advanced-level Idea List using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: Portfolio Building to Solve No Project Ideas
content.seo_description: Stop searching for inspiration. Learn how to build a high-converting portfolio using Antigravity and Claude Code to solve the no project ideas dilemma.
content.answer_block: The 'no project ideas' trap is a failure of market observation, not creativity. Solve this by building vertical SaaS micro-tools that solve specific, high-friction B2B workflows. By leveraging AI-assisted scaffolding like Antigravity and Claude Code, you can ship a production-ready MVP in under 48 hours to validate revenue.
content.intro:
You stare at a blank IDE, the cursor blinking with mocking precision. The market is saturated with clone apps, and your ambition is paralyzed by the lack of a 'million-dollar' spark. This isn't a lack of talent; it is a lack of systematic framework for identifying friction in the wild.

True founders don't wait for lightning to strike. They hunt for inefficiencies in existing workflows and automate them into existence. We are moving beyond toy projects. We are building a portfolio that functions as a lead-generation machine, using advanced AI-driven tooling to compress months of development into a single weekend.

steps.title: Identify High-LTV Micro-Problems
steps.description: Scan niche subreddits and G2 reviews for 'manual entry' complaints. Pick a problem where someone is currently paying for a bloated tool but only needs one specific feature.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the provided market feedback transcript. Extract 3 high-friction, repeatable tasks that can be solved with a single-purpose micro-SaaS. Prioritize tasks with high manual labor cost.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Analyze market-feedback.txt and output a JSON list of 3 MVP opportunities'.

steps.title: Architect the Data Schema
steps.description: Define your database structure for scalability. Don't over-engineer; focus on a relational schema that supports rapid iteration.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a Prisma schema for a multi-tenant SaaS application that handles user authentication, billing, and the core workflow identified in step 1.
steps.claude_code_command: claude write 'Create prisma/schema.prisma with User, Subscription, and CoreWorkflow models.'

steps.title: Scaffold the Core Logic
steps.description: Use your AI tools to build the 'hard' part of your app first. Never start with the UI; start with the API and business logic.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write the backend service layer for the core workflow. Include error handling, input validation, and optimized database queries.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Implement the main service logic in src/services/core-workflow.ts'

steps.title: Implement High-Conversion UI
steps.description: Build a functional, clean interface using Tailwind. Focus on the 'Time to Value'—how fast can the user get their first result?
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a responsive React component for the user dashboard. Focus on a minimalist UI that highlights the core value proposition immediately upon login.
steps.claude_code_command: claude write 'Create src/components/Dashboard.tsx using Tailwind CSS for a modern, clean look.'

steps.title: Deploy and Integrate Billing
steps.description: A project without a checkout button is just a hobby. Integrate Stripe and deploy to a production environment.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Provide a guide and code snippets to integrate Stripe Checkout for a subscription-based model within a Next.js application.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Setup stripe-webhook.ts and integrate checkout flow in app/api/checkout/route.ts'

pro_tips: Build for 'Hair on Fire' problems where the user is already spending money on a broken process.
Prioritize 'Time to First Value' (TTFV) over feature count; if the user doesn't see a result in 60 seconds, they will churn.
Use Claude Code to write your unit tests concurrently with your features to ensure long-term maintainability.
Ship the landing page and payment link before you finish the backend; if nobody buys, don't build the features.
Treat your GitHub repo as a public ledger of your engineering competence; document your decision-making process in the README.
content.next_steps: Once the MVP is live, implement a feedback loop by adding a simple 'report bug' or 'request feature' widget. Use that data to iterate your next feature set based on actual user demand rather than assumptions.
content.conversion_block: You now have the framework to transform your technical skills into a revenue-generating portfolio. Stop coding for practice and start coding for impact. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
